digest.greeting: Monday, June 22, Jean. Today is anchored by the morning timecard deadline, TRACE & Staff Aug, EPMO Portfolio Review, the Mental Health project touchpoint, and the Send Letters Queue checkpoint. Outlook shows 7 calendar blocks, 2 carried-over priorities, 0 active reminders, and 3 urgent items. The day should stay focused on approving pending timecards before the payroll cutoff, turning the EPMO/KPI work into leadership-ready decisions, and using the Staff Aug, Mental Health, Billing intake, and SLQ checkpoints to lock clear next steps. Teams broad-thread discovery returned a Microsoft Graph 403, so Teams context is limited to readable unread chats and direct chat fetches.

items.label: Finish the EPMO storyline for leadership use
items.detail: Still open from recent digests. Keep the story tight around proof points, current risks, and what leadership needs to decide or unblock across Staff Augmentation, Send Letters, CoE metrics, and active intake movement.

items.label: Turn the KPI set into SMART goals
items.detail: The KPI-to-SMART-goals reminder is still open and has a saved priority reaction. Convert the KPI set into a small set of clear goals that can support the EPMO storyline and PM/PE follow-through.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times shown in Jean's Outlook working timezone. The day has an 8:00 AM admin block, a short TRACE/Staff Aug touchpoint, a compressed 11:00-12:00 leadership/intake stretch, and a late-day Send Letters checkpoint.

items.label: Approve Timecards & Prepare for the Week
items.detail: This aligns with the Rippling alerts requiring pending timecards to be reviewed by 12:00 PM EST on Mondays.

items.label: TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint
items.detail: Use this as the live checkpoint for Staff Augmentation follow-through after last week's action-plan work and the unread meeting chat closure.

items.label: 1:1 Shelley & Jean
items.detail: Department-needs/support conversation. Capture any support asks before the EPMO Portfolio Review immediately after.

items.label: EPMO Portfolio Review
items.detail: Review new project requests and assign next steps. The Billing intake and Mental Health project signals are the likeliest items to route cleanly here.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Best focus window for EPMO storyline cleanup, KPI-to-SMART-goals drafting, or Staff Aug action-plan notes before the afternoon.

items.label: Exciting NEW project touchpoint re/Mental Health
items.detail: Prep-worthy new-project discussion with Mar Garcia. Use the recent Asana Mental Health follow-on items to clarify scope, training/change-management needs, and the next owner.

items.label: Send Letters Queue Touchpoint
items.detail: Late-day checkpoint for SLQ 2.0. The Baesman packaging thread now has answers on daily cadence, envelope options, sizes, and scenario assumptions.

items.label: Approve pending Rippling timecards before noon ET
items.detail: Rippling sent multiple unread alerts for the June 1-30 pay period, including Mar Garcia, Andrea Rodriguez, and David Molano. The admin note says pending timecards should be reviewed by 12:00 PM EST on Mondays to avoid payroll delays or discrepancies.

items.label: Use the TRACE & Staff Aug touchpoint to close open asks
items.detail: The prior Staff Aug item is marked complete in state, but the unread meeting chat ended with Evan asking what, if anything, is still needed from him. Use the 9:30 AM touchpoint to confirm whether any support ask remains before this thread fades.

items.label: Route the new Billing intake before or during Portfolio Review
items.detail: Janet McDowell sent a New Intake Alert for Billing from Shannon Cooper. Decide whether it belongs in today's EPMO Portfolio Review queue and assign the next intake owner if it needs movement this week.

items.label: Prepare the Mental Health project next-step discussion
items.detail: The afternoon Mental Health project touchpoint should clarify scope, change-management/training needs, and who owns immediate follow-on actions from the related Asana activity. Bring this into the meeting as a decision path, not just a status update.

items.label: Confirm the SLQ 2.0 Baesman path at 4 PM
items.detail: The Baesman packaging thread has useful detail: daily sends, envelope supply scenarios, trifold and manila sizing, and pricing/timeline needs. Use the Send Letters Queue touchpoint to confirm what is still needed from Procurement, IT, or the project team.

items.label: Rippling timecard alerts are still unread
items.detail: Three high-signal unread alerts point to unapproved timecards and a Monday payroll cutoff. This is already captured as the top action, so treat the email thread as the source reminder rather than a separate workflow.

items.label: New Billing intake needs routing clarity
items.detail: The Janet McDowell alert names Shannon Cooper as submitter and Billing as the department. Review the Asana-linked intake before the 11:30 AM Portfolio Review if it needs assignment today.

items.label: SLQ 2.0 packaging thread has fresh vendor context
items.detail: Teresa Moya-Perez acknowledged the answers Jhara provided on packaging specs. If pricing, minimums, or onboarding timeline are still missing, make that the concrete follow-up for the 4:00 PM SLQ checkpoint.

items.label: Clarify Bucket 6 ownership risk for Incident Response
items.detail: Lucia said she can try to fill the reputation/brand matrix next week, but she is not fully involved in PR right now and noted a new director may start in about three weeks. Decide whether to wait, assign an interim reviewer, or narrow what is needed for the June 30 draft path.

items.label: 1:1 Jessica and Jean
items.detail: Useful follow-up point for Case Change or process-map delivery if Jessica's Tuesday deliverables need a checkpoint.

items.label: 1:1 Jhara & Jean
items.detail: Agenda already names portfolio review, roadblocks, and next steps. Good place to pressure-test SLQ, Mental Health, and active PM work.

items.label: Blocked Space
items.detail: Keep this available for EPMO storyline cleanup or follow-through from today's Portfolio Review.

items.label: bi-weekly sync (J & J)
items.detail: Workload, challenges, wins, and goals sync with Janet McDowell.

items.label: LeadUp Module 2 and 1:1 Yuri overlap
items.detail: Outlook shows LeadUp Module 2 and 1:1 Yuri & Jean starting at the same time. Decide coverage before tomorrow afternoon.
